slides.title: eBay Mitchell Parts Audit
slides.subtitle:
Inventory risk review for Tony's Mitchell fishing reel parts listings
Validated discrepancy detection with a clear path to productization

slides.title: Business Problem and Why It Matters
bullets: Tony has multiple eBay listings that can contain the same Mitchell fishing reel parts.
eBay does not reconcile shared inventory across those listings.
That creates oversell risk when the same part appears available in more than one listing.
The audit is valuable because it turns hidden cross-listing inventory conflicts into a reviewable operational workflow.

slides.title: What the Audit Already Accomplished
bullets: Tony explicitly confirmed that the audit found real discrepancies.
He said it likely prevented selling a part that was actually out of stock.
This is not a speculative prototype story, the work has already delivered operational value.
The project has effectively validated the need for a repeatable internal inventory risk capability.

slides.title: Proof Metrics from Current Runs
bullets: Initial live run: 2 listings scanned, 111 part rows, 12 overlapping parts, 25 mismatch rows, 37 warnings.
Larger batch run: 15 listings scanned, 998 part rows, 61 overlapping parts, 135 mismatch rows, 553 warnings.
Summary notes indicate some sample output used mock data plus approved quantity-state rules.
Live browser extraction also faced challenge-gating in the current environment, but the discrepancy signal was still useful and business value was validated.

slides.title: Next Phase: Mitchell Inventory Risk Console
bullets: Productize the audit into a desktop-friendly internal dashboard for Tony.
Core views should include Audit Overview, Parts Needing Attention, Listing View, listing detail, and part-level drilldown.
The system should preserve official run history, separate official runs from test or dev output, and support a monthly default cadence.
This phase should improve reviewability, prioritization, reporting, and operational flow, without making automatic eBay edits.
